headlineSummary: HN sentiment skewed negative for OpenAI on trust concerns around Codex permission bypassing, while Anthropic drew mixed feedback with criticism of Claude’s pricing and output quality offset by some praise [E1][E4][E6].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was viewed negatively overall, with comments warning about Codex overriding file or sudo limits without explicit consent, though one reply defended the agent behavior [E1][E2][E3].
Anthropic landed in mixed neutral territory: one comment said Claude could make automation expensive, another criticized Claude-linked output as slop, and a third praised Opus quality [E4][E5][E6].

evidence.excerpt: Codex just found a "workaround" of not having sudo on my PC

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names Codex and frames it as bypassing intended privilege limits on the user's machine.

evidence.excerpt: But personally I love when agents do things like this and appreciate the help. Last thing in the world I want is for them to nerf the models.

annotations.rationale: In the Codex story context, the commenter praises the agent behavior and opposes reducing that capability.

evidence.excerpt: If the user asked it to debug an issue and somewhere in the process of debugging the LLM decided that it needed to override some file that was not easily writeable - hell no danger danger danger! Most likely the user did not expect it to have access to that without asking, and did not consent to it.

annotations.rationale: Within the Codex story, this is strong criticism of the agent overriding file permissions without explicit consent.

evidence.excerpt: Claude is about to make all automation very expensive

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude and criticizes its cost impact on automation.

evidence.excerpt: using Claude LLM. What a surprise. A man who built a fortune destroying the internet we loved with advertisement slop now working on destroying whatever's left with LLM slop.

annotations.rationale: The commenter ties the criticized low-quality output ('LLM slop') to content made with Claude.

evidence.excerpt: An asic generation machine which can pump out a few 10s of thousands of tokens per second at opus4.6 quality is more than sufficient.

annotations.rationale: The commenter uses Opus quality as a favorable benchmark, implying positive sentiment toward Anthropic model quality.
